introSection: Aftershock is a steel inverted shuttle roller coaster built by Vekoma. It is located at Silverwood Theme Park in Athol, Idaho, USA. The ride formerly operated at Six Flags Great America in Gurnee, Illinois, USA, under the name Déjà Vu from 2001 to 2007. This is the only Vekoma Giant Inverted Boomerang currently operating in the United States.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Déjà Vu opened on October 6, 2001 at Six Flags Great America. It replaced Sky Whirl, which was removed the previous year.

Near the end of the 2007 season, rumors began circulating that Déjà Vu would be removed. It closed on October 28, 2007 after it was confirmed to coaster enthusiasts on its last day of operation. It was later replaced by Buccaneer Battle in 2009.

The roller coaster was later listed up for sale by Six Flags on International Rides Management in November 2007, alongside an identical copy at Six Flags Over Georgia. Its removal started later that month.

By the end of the month, Déjà Vu was sold to Silverwood Theme Park. This was confirmed in December 2007.

Dismantling continued in early January 2008 at Six Flags Great America, with the removal of the catch cars.

On January 24, 2008, Silverwood Theme Park officially announced that the roller coaster would be added to the park. An naming contest was also announced along with an July opening.

On February 15, 2008, track removal for Déjà Vu started in preparation for relocation to its new location with the vertical loop being removed.

In March 2008, pieces of Déjà Vu started to arrive at Silverwood Theme Park. Also that month, the name of the roller coaster was revealed as Aftershock, following a competition to name the coaster.

Construction of Aftershock started in April 2008. The cobra roll was nearly completed by May 9. By late April, only a few pieces of Déjà Vu's towers remained on site at Six Flags Great America.

On May 20, 2008, the vertical loop and the rest of the cobra roll were completed.

It opened on July 21, 2008.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Light green track and blue supports.

fullText:
Aftershock is a steel inverted shuttle roller coaster built by Vekoma. It is located at Silverwood Theme Park in Athol, Idaho, USA. The ride formerly operated at Six Flags Great America in Gurnee, Illinois, USA, under the name Déjà Vu from 2001 to 2007. This is the only Vekoma Giant Inverted Boomerang currently operating in the United States.

Déjà Vu opened on October 6, 2001 at Six Flags Great America. It replaced Sky Whirl, which was removed the previous year.

Near the end of the 2007 season, rumors began circulating that Déjà Vu would be removed. It closed on October 28, 2007 after it was confirmed to coaster enthusiasts on its last day of operation. It was later replaced by Buccaneer Battle in 2009.

The roller coaster was later listed up for sale by Six Flags on International Rides Management in November 2007, alongside an identical copy at Six Flags Over Georgia. Its removal started later that month.

By the end of the month, Déjà Vu was sold to Silverwood Theme Park. This was confirmed in December 2007.

Dismantling continued in early January 2008 at Six Flags Great America, with the removal of the catch cars.

On January 24, 2008, Silverwood Theme Park officially announced that the roller coaster would be added to the park. An naming contest was also announced along with an July opening.

On February 15, 2008, track removal for Déjà Vu started in preparation for relocation to its new location with the vertical loop being removed.

In March 2008, pieces of Déjà Vu started to arrive at Silverwood Theme Park. Also that month, the name of the roller coaster was revealed as Aftershock, following a competition to name the coaster.

Construction of Aftershock started in April 2008. The cobra roll was nearly completed by May 9. By late April, only a few pieces of Déjà Vu's towers remained on site at Six Flags Great America.

On May 20, 2008, the vertical loop and the rest of the cobra roll were completed.

It opened on July 21, 2008.



Light green track and blue supports.
